title: Add IPv6 byte-count comment to socks5_helper.rs for ATYP=4
description: socks5_helper.rs comments the RFC 1928 frame formats clearly (lines 44, 60), including ATYP=1 (IPv4: 4 bytes + 2 port) and ATYP=3 (domain: 1-byte-length + N bytes + 2 port). Line 8 mentions 'ATYP=4 (IPv6) is rejected', but doesn't document the byte layout of IPv6 addresses (16 bytes + 2 port). When a contributor adds IPv6 support, they need this info to avoid off-by-one errors in the read buffer size. Add a comment on line 8: 'ATYP=4 (IPv6) is rejected — would require 16-byte address + 2-byte port.'
